Water moves through a repeating cycle around the world. </s>
Sunlight warms water in rivers, lakes, and the ocean. </s>
Warm water changes into water vapor. </s>
Evaporation changes warm water into water vapor. </s>
Water vapor rises into the air. </s>
Cooling changes water vapor into small droplets. </s>
Condensation changes water vapor into droplets. </s>
Heavy water droplets fall from clouds as rain. </s>
Water falling from clouds is precipitation. </s>
Rainwater flows downhill over the ground. </s>
Rainwater collects in rivers and lakes. </s>
Rivers carry water to the ocean. </s>
The water cycle repeats after water reaches the ocean. </s>
Sunlight keeps the water cycle moving. </s>
